# Plan: v1.1 Phase 10 — Infrastructure

**Source roadmap:** `plans/design-system-v1.1-roadmap.md` § Phase 10
**Branch:** `v1.1/phase-10-infrastructure`
**Depends on:** none hard-blocked. Best run LAST so auto-generated
token docs and visual regression baselines capture the final v1.1
state.

---

## Context

ln-acme v1.0 ships without:
- Auto-generated token reference (docs drift from source)
- Visual regression testing (consumers find regressions in prod)
- CHANGELOG (no way to scan what's new in a release)
- Migration guide discipline (v1.1 has breaking changes)
- Conventional Commit enforcement (optional)

Phase 10 adds the development-experience layer. No runtime behavior
changes — consumers pulling in v1.1 without any of this see zero
difference. It's about **us**, not them.

## Pre-resolved ambiguities

### A. Auto-generated token docs

Parse `scss/config/_tokens.scss`, extract every `--*:` declaration
with its comment, and generate `docs/css/tokens.generated.md`. The
existing `docs/css/tokens.md` stays as the narrative doc; the
generated file is the exhaustive reference.

Tool: a small Node script `tools/generate-token-docs.mjs`. Run via
`npm run docs:tokens`.

Parser scope: simple regex over `--name: value; // comment` lines.
Nothing fancy — the token file is flat, no Sass logic inside the
`:root` block.

### B. Playwright visual regression

Scope: every `demo/admin/*.html` page. One snapshot per page at
`1440×900`. Baselines stored in `tests/visual/__snapshots__/`.

Tolerance: 1% pixel delta per snapshot (matches the Phase 2 tolerance
decision from the roadmap). Anything over 1% fails CI and requires
human review.

Setup:
- Install `@playwright/test` as dev dependency
- `tests/visual/demo-pages.spec.ts` — for each demo page, navigate,
  wait for fonts, take snapshot
- `playwright.config.ts` — single browser (Chromium), headless, no
  JS concurrency issues
- `npm run test:visual` — run suite
- `npm run test:visual:update` — regenerate baselines (for
  intentional changes)

Run once after Phase 10 lands to generate the initial baseline.
Subsequent phases run it in CI.

### C. CHANGELOG

Keep-a-Changelog format. Start at `[1.1.0] - YYYY-MM-DD` with
sections:

- Added
- Changed
- Deprecated
- Removed
- Fixed
- Security

Entries per phase, not per commit. Add a "Breaking changes" callout
at the top of v1.1 section listing the neutral-scale remap,
typography role tokens, and focus-ring signature change.

### D. Migration guide

`docs/migrations/v1.0-to-v1.1.md`. For each breaking change, before/
after code snippet + rationale. Reference the phase that introduced
it.

### E. Commit-lint

Out of scope for Phase 10. The existing commit discipline is already
reliable (every Phase used Conventional Commits). Skip.

### F. Interactive token explorer

Defer. Nice-to-have, not v1.1 critical. Put in `backlog.md`.

### G. Commit strategy

Four commits:

1. `feat(docs): add auto-generated token reference`
2. `feat(tests): add Playwright visual regression suite`
3. `feat(docs): add CHANGELOG.md with v1.1 entries`
4. `feat(docs): add v1.0 → v1.1 migration guide`

## Executor Prompt

### Prerequisites

1. `CLAUDE.md`
2. `package.json` (current)
3. `scss/config/_tokens.scss` (final v1.1 state)
4. `plans/design-system-v1.1-roadmap.md` (for the Changed/Added lists)

### Step 1 — Branch

```bash
git checkout main
git pull
git checkout -b v1.1/phase-10-infrastructure
```

**Run this LAST.** Phases 1–9 should already be in main.

### Step 2 — Auto-generated token docs (Commit 1)

Create `tools/generate-token-docs.mjs`:

```javascript
import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
import { dirname, join } from 'node:path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';

const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const TOKENS_PATH = join(__dirname, '../scss/config/_tokens.scss');
const OUTPUT_PATH = join(__dirname, '../docs/css/tokens.generated.md');

const source = readFileSync(TOKENS_PATH, 'utf8');

// Parse --name: value; (// inline comment)? blocks, grouped by
// preceding `// Section name` comments.
const groups = [];
let currentGroup = null;

for (const rawLine of source.split('\n')) {
	const line = rawLine.trim();

	// Section header like `// Colors — Primary`
	const sectionMatch = line.match(/^\/\/\s*-{3,}.*$/);
	if (sectionMatch) continue; // divider

	const headerMatch = line.match(/^\/\/\s*([A-Z][^\n]*)$/);
	if (headerMatch && !line.includes('--')) {
		currentGroup = { title: headerMatch[1].trim(), tokens: [] };
		groups.push(currentGroup);
		continue;
	}

	const tokenMatch = line.match(/^(--[a-z0-9-]+)\s*:\s*([^;]+);(?:\s*\/\/\s*(.*))?/);
	if (tokenMatch && currentGroup) {
		currentGroup.tokens.push({
			name: tokenMatch[1],
			value: tokenMatch[2].trim(),
			comment: tokenMatch[3] ? tokenMatch[3].trim() : '',
		});
	}
}

// Emit Markdown
let md = '# Tokens — Auto-generated reference\n\n';
md += '> Generated from `scss/config/_tokens.scss` by `tools/generate-token-docs.mjs`. Do not edit.\n\n';

for (const group of groups) {
	if (group.tokens.length === 0) continue;
	md += `## ${group.title}\n\n`;
	md += '| Token | Value | Note |\n';
	md += '|---|---|---|\n';
	for (const { name, value, comment } of group.tokens) {
		md += `| \`${name}\` | \`${value}\` | ${comment || ''} |\n`;
	}
	md += '\n';
}

writeFileSync(OUTPUT_PATH, md, 'utf8');
console.log(`Wrote ${OUTPUT_PATH}`);
```

Add to `package.json` scripts:

```json
{
	"scripts": {
		"docs:tokens": "node tools/generate-token-docs.mjs"
	}
}
```

Run:

```bash
npm run docs:tokens
```

Expected: creates `docs/css/tokens.generated.md` with every token
from `_tokens.scss`.

**Commit 1:**

```bash
git add tools/generate-token-docs.mjs \
        docs/css/tokens.generated.md \
        package.json
git commit -m "feat(docs): add auto-generated token reference"
```

### Step 3 — Playwright visual regression (Commit 2)

Install Playwright:

```bash
npm install --save-dev @playwright/test
npx playwright install chromium
```

Create `playwright.config.ts`:

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
	testDir: './tests/visual',
	fullyParallel: false,
	workers: 1,
	retries: 0,
	use: {
		baseURL: 'http://localhost:4173',
		viewport: { width: 1440, height: 900 },
	},
	projects: [
		{
			name: 'chromium',
			use: { browserName: 'chromium' },
		},
	],
	webServer: {
		command: 'npm run preview',
		url: 'http://localhost:4173',
		reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
	},
	expect: {
		toHaveScreenshot: {
			maxDiffPixelRatio: 0.01,   // 1% tolerance
		},
	},
});
```

Create `tests/visual/demo-pages.spec.ts`:

```typescript
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';
import { readdirSync } from 'node:fs';
import { join } from 'node:path';

const demoDir = join(__dirname, '../../demo/admin');
const pages = readdirSync(demoDir)
	.filter((f) => f.endsWith('.html'))
	.map((f) => f.replace('.html', ''));

for (const page of pages) {
	test(`demo/admin/${page}`, async ({ page: browserPage }) => {
		await browserPage.goto(`/demo/admin/${page}.html`);
		await browserPage.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
		// Wait for web fonts to render
		await browserPage.waitForFunction(() => document.fonts.ready);
		await expect(browserPage).toHaveScreenshot(`${page}.png`, {
			fullPage: true,
		});
	});
}
```

Add scripts to `package.json`:

```json
{
	"scripts": {
		"test:visual": "playwright test",
		"test:visual:update": "playwright test --update-snapshots",
		"preview": "vite preview --port 4173"
	}
}
```

(Assumes `vite` as build tool — adjust to actual build tool if
different. Check `package.json` for current setup.)

Generate baseline:

```bash
npm run build
npm run test:visual:update
```

Verify baselines exist in `tests/visual/demo-pages.spec.ts-snapshots/`
or equivalent Playwright path. Commit the baselines alongside the
test.

Run a second time to confirm green:

```bash
npm run test:visual
```

Expected: all pages pass (self-test).

**Commit 2:**

```bash
git add playwright.config.ts \
        tests/visual/ \
        package.json \
        package-lock.json
git commit -m "feat(tests): add Playwright visual regression suite for demo pages"
```

### Step 4 — CHANGELOG (Commit 3)

Create `CHANGELOG.md`:

```markdown
# Changelog

All notable changes to ln-acme are documented here. Format based on
[Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/). This project
adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [1.1.0] — YYYY-MM-DD

### Breaking changes

- **Neutral color scale (Phase 2)** — `--color-text-primary`,
  `--color-bg-body`, `--color-border` etc. now reference
  `var(--color-neutral-*)`. Most consumers see no visual change;
  consumers that read bare HSL triplets directly may see ≤1% drift.
- **Typography role tokens (Phase 3)** — new `typography($role)`
  mixin. The existing `text-xs` → `text-2xl` primitives are
  unchanged, but `scss/base/_typography.scss` now maps `h1`–`h6` to
  role tokens. Consumers that override headings may see small shifts.
- **Focus-ring mixin signature (Phase 4)** — the `$width` parameter
  was removed. The ring is now a fixed three-layer halo.
- **Shadow token values (Phase 4)** — all `--shadow-*` values were
  re-tuned to a cool-tinted hue. The *structure* of shadows is
  unchanged but pixels differ.

See `docs/migrations/v1.0-to-v1.1.md` for migration steps.

### Added

- Foundation tokens (Phase 1): `$bp-sm` through `$bp-3xl` Sass vars,
  `$cq-narrow` through `$cq-wide` container-query vars, `--spacing-2xs`
  / `3xl` / `4xl` / `5xl`, `--max-w-prose`, `--max-w-form`,
  `--max-w-content`, `--max-w-container`, `--easing-standard` /
  `-decelerate` / `-accelerate` / `-spring`, `--border-width-strong`.
  New `motion-safe` mixin.
- Neutral color scale `--color-neutral-50` through `900` (Phase 2).
- Typography semantic roles (Phase 3): `display-lg` / `display-md` /
  `display-sm` / `heading-lg` / `heading-md` / `heading-sm` /
  `title-md` / `title-sm` / `body-lg` / `body-md` / `body-sm` /
  `label-md` / `label-sm` / `caption`. New `typography($role)` mixin.
- New shadow tokens: `--shadow-2xl`, `--shadow-inner`,
  `--shadow-success`, `--shadow-error` (Phase 4).
- Dark mode via `[data-theme="dark"]` and `prefers-color-scheme: dark`
  auto-apply (Phase 6). Opt-out via `[data-theme="light"]`.
- CSS components (Phase 7): `empty-state`, `page-header`,
  `stepper`, `timeline`, `stat-card`, `prose`, `kbd`, `tooltip`
  (CSS baseline), `toggle-switch`, `chip`, `filter-toolbar`.
- JS components (Phase 8): `ln-tooltip`, `ln-popover`, `ln-copy`,
  `ln-shortcuts`.
- Density system (Phase 9): `[data-density="compact"]` scoped
  spacing + typography override.
- Infrastructure (Phase 10): auto-generated token docs, Playwright
  visual regression suite, CHANGELOG, migration guide.

### Changed

- Every animation and transform/height transition is gated behind
  `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference` (Phase 5).
- `table-base` now sets `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` (Phase 3).
- `focus-ring` mixin produces a 3-layer halo (Phase 4).

### Fixed

- Literal `rem` values in `_table.scss` and `_form.scss` replaced with
  `var(--spacing-*)` so density mode can scale them (Phase 9 audit).

## [1.0.1] — 2026-03-XX

(Previous release — unchanged.)
```

Fill in dates as the phase is landed.

**Commit 3:**

```bash
git add CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "feat(docs): add CHANGELOG.md with v1.1 entries"
```

### Step 5 — Migration guide (Commit 4)

Create `docs/migrations/v1.0-to-v1.1.md`:

```markdown
# Migrating from ln-acme v1.0 to v1.1

This guide covers every breaking change in v1.1. If your project only
consumes tokens via the `hsl(var(--token))` pattern and uses the
documented mixins, you likely need zero changes — v1.1 is
intentionally compatible.

## Breaking changes

### 1. Neutral color scale (Phase 2)

**What changed:** `--color-text-primary`, `--color-bg-body`, etc. now
resolve through `var(--color-neutral-*)` instead of holding bare
HSL triplets.

**Who needs to change:** consumers that read the bare HSL triplet
directly, e.g. `color: hsl(var(--color-text-primary) / 0.5);`. Still
works — the indirection resolves correctly. But if you were
programmatically parsing the token value in JS (e.g. for a theme
switcher), expect to see `var(--color-neutral-900)` instead of
`221 39% 11%`.

**Migration:** read the resolved value via `getComputedStyle()`
instead of parsing the raw `CSSStyleSheet` declarations.

### 2. Typography role tokens (Phase 3)

**What changed:** new `--text-display-*` / `--text-heading-*` / etc.
tokens. `h1`–`h6` in `scss/base/_typography.scss` now use
`@include typography(role)`. The `text-xs` → `text-2xl` primitives
are unchanged.

**Who needs to change:** consumers with heading overrides that assume
a specific primitive. Example:

```scss
// Before (v1.0)
h1 { @include text-2xl; }  // 1.5rem

// After (v1.1) — primitive still works
h1 { @include text-2xl; }  // still 1.5rem

// Preferred v1.1 style
h1 { @include typography(display-sm); }  // 2.25rem — the new default
```

**Migration:** no forced change. Optionally migrate to role tokens
for semantic clarity.

### 3. focus-ring mixin signature (Phase 4)

**What changed:** the `$width` parameter was removed. The new mixin
produces a fixed three-layer halo (2px separator + 2px color + 2px
glow, 6px total).

**Who needs to change:** consumers that called `focus-ring($width:
2px)` or similar.

**Migration:** drop the `$width` argument. If you need a different
thickness, use `focus-combination`, `focus-inset-shadow`, or write a
custom mixin.

```scss
// Before
input:focus-visible { @include focus-ring(var(--color-primary), 2px); }

// After
input:focus-visible { @include focus-ring(var(--color-primary)); }
```

### 4. Shadow values (Phase 4)

**What changed:** all `--shadow-xs` / `sm` / `md` / `lg` / `xl` values
were re-tuned. The structure is the same (two-layer dual drop) but
pixels differ — cool-tinted hue, slightly different alphas.

**Who needs to change:** consumers with pixel-perfect snapshot tests.

**Migration:** regenerate snapshots. If the new shadows look wrong in
your project, override at `:root`:

```scss
:root {
	--shadow-sm: /* your old values */;
}
```

## No change needed

The following are **additions only**, safe to ignore if not needed:

- New spacing steps (`2xs`, `3xl`, `4xl`, `5xl`)
- Content-width tokens
- Named easings (`--easing-*`)
- `motion-safe` mixin
- Dark mode (opt-in)
- New components (empty-state, page-header, etc.)
- Density system
- `tabular-nums` on tables (cosmetic improvement)

## Recommended

After upgrading, consider:

1. Replacing `text-*` primitives in heading overrides with
   `typography(<role>)` calls.
2. Wrapping long-running animations in `motion-safe`.
3. Testing under `prefers-color-scheme: dark`.
4. For data-dense views, adding `data-density="compact"` to the
   section root and removing hand-rolled padding overrides.

## Rollback

If v1.1 breaks your project, pin to v1.0.1 in `package.json`:

```json
"ln-acme": "1.0.1"
```

Then file an issue with a reproduction.
```

Add a link from `README.md` near the top:

```markdown
> **Upgrading from v1.0?** See `docs/migrations/v1.0-to-v1.1.md` and
> `CHANGELOG.md`.
```

**Commit 4:**

```bash
git add docs/migrations/v1.0-to-v1.1.md README.md
git commit -m "feat(docs): add v1.0 → v1.1 migration guide"
```

### Step 6 — Verification

```bash
npm run build
npm run docs:tokens
npm run test:visual
git log --oneline main..HEAD
```

Expected: 4 commits, all verify commands green.

### Boundaries — do NOT touch

- Any runtime code (`scss/`, `js/` source).
- Any existing documentation (beyond the README link).
- Commit-lint / Husky setup (out of scope).
- Interactive token explorer (deferred to v1.2).
